data.title: Brambles U.S. Margins Could Be Pressured by Lumber Inflation — Market Talk
data.description: Pallet giant Brambles's U.S. earnings margins could come under pressure from increased lumber prices, Jarden analysts say. They say in a note that lumber costs are reaccelerating at a time when pallet prices are stuck in reverse. Their analysis suggests a 10% on-year rise in lumber costs so far in Brambles' fiscal second half, compared with a 1.8% on-year decline in April for pallet prices. All told, they see broad near-term challenges for Brambles but maintain a neutral rating and its target price of A$19.80 on the stock. Shares are down 0.1% at A$22.13. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: NetEase H-Share Target Price Raised to HK$235.00 From HK$210.00 by Daiwa >NTES
Pierer Mobility's Majority Shareholder Enters Call Option for Future Change of Control
data.description:
Pierer Mobility (PKTM.VI, ) said its majority shareholder, Pierer Bajaj, entered into a call option that allows a change of control in the Austrian motorcycle and e-bikes manufacturer.

Pierer Industrie, which holds a 50.1% stake in Pierer Bajaj, concluded the call option with Bajaj Auto International Holdings, which holds the remaining 49.9% stake, according to a Thursday release.

The agreement allows Bajaj Auto International to gain indirect control over Pierer Mobility until the end of May 2026 by acquiring Pierer Industrie's shares in Pierer Bajaj.

The company noted that the move would require regulatory approvals.

data.title: Sunway Likely Has Clear Earnings Visibility With New Projects — Market Talk
data.description: Sunway Bhd. likely has clear earnings visibility due to its new development projects, RHB IB analyst Loong Kok Wen writes in a note. The conglomerate has also secured construction contracts, she says. Sunway's 1Q property sales of MYR554.7 million is on track to achieve its target of MYR3.6 billion by the year-end, she adds. New launches worth a total of MYR4.1 billion will be rolled out this year, she notes. RHB maintains the stock's buy rating and a target price of MYR5.79. Shares are 1.7% lower at MYR4.75.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: China 10-Year Yield Rises
data.description:
China’s 10-year government bond yield rose to around 1.70% on Friday, as investors shifted toward riskier assets following news that the US and China had agreed to sustain diplomatic dialogue amid persistent tensions across various fronts.

According to China’s Foreign Ministry, Vice Foreign Minister Ma Zhaoxu and US Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau held a phone call on Thursday to discuss key bilateral issues, a conversation later confirmed by the US State Department.

Investors viewed the renewed diplomatic engagement as a notable step toward managing the complex relationship between the two global powers.

Meanwhile, China’s central bank injected CNY 500 billion into the banking system via its one-year medium-term lending facility, aiming to maintain ample liquidity and support credit growth amid a fragile economic recovery.

The move follows the PBOC’s first key lending rate cut in seven months, lowering the one-year LPR to 3.0% and the five-year to 3.5%.

data.title: New Zealand Households' Inflation Expectations Fall in June Quarter
data.description:
New Zealand households' inflation expectations fell in the June quarter, while expectations for higher house prices grew, according to a survey published by the Reserve Bank of New Zealand on Friday.

New Zealand households' perception of current inflation for the June quarter slipped to a mean of 6.9% from 7.7% in the March quarter and 7.9% a year earlier.

Mean expectations for one-year inflation jumped to 5.6% in the quarter from 4.9% in the previous quarter. Two-year inflation expectations climbed to 4.7% from 3.9%, while five-year inflation expectations edged higher to 3.7% from 3.6%.

Mean expectations for one-year house prices declined to 2.2% in the June quarter from 2.7% in the March quarter, while those for the five-year house price inflation inched lower to 7.3% from 8%, the report showed.

data.title: Italy - Factors to watch on May 23
data.description:
The following factors could affect Italian markets on Friday.

Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy. New items are marked with (*).

For a complete list of diary events in Italy please click on .

POLITICS

Nine European countries, led by Italy and Denmark, called on Thursday for the European Union to make it simpler for member states to expel foreign criminals. The countries signed a letter which was presented to journalists at a Rome news conference held by Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and her Danish counterpart Mette Frederiksen.

ECONOMY

Italy is confident the United States will agree to a trade deal with the European Union similar to the one Washington has already struck with Britain, Economy Minister Giancarlo Giorgetti said on Thursday.

Italy's defence spending is slightly above NATO's target of 2% of gross domestic product target, Giorgetti said. He added that the European Union should work on ways to promote the defence industry through the involvement of private investors.

Italy fully supports European Union efforts to stop Russian gas imports into the bloc by 2027, the country's Energy minister told reporters on Thursday, adding any decisions by Rome to boost LNG imports from the U.S. were up to private buyers.

DEBT

Moody's and Scope rating agencies conduct scheduled reviews of Italy's sovereign debt rating.

Italy's Treasury announces sale of 6-month BOT bills, with relative amounts to be auctioned on May 28.

The Treasury said on Thursday it would sell up to 4.75 billion euros ($5.36 billion) in short-term and inflation-linked BTP bonds at May 27 auction.

COMPANIES

GENERALI

Italy's top insurer Generali appointed on Thursday Deloitte Financial Advisory and law firm Advant Ntcm as advisers for the bid launched by merchant bank Mediobanca for the private bank Banca Generali .

ENEL

Shareholders of Italy's biggest utility on Thursday approved a buyback plan worth up to 3.5 billion euros and the possibility to cancel acquired shares without reducing its capital, opening the way for the company to increase its investor reward.

BPER BANCA , BANCA POPOLARE DI SONDRIO

Bper Banca said on Thursday the European Central Bank had approved the Italian lender's share capital increase to service its bid for local peer Banca Popolare di Sondrio.

AZIMUT

The Italian asset manager said on Thursday it had signed a binding agreement with domestic private equity fund FSI to create a fintech bank, spinning off a portion of its financial adviser network into the new entity.

TELECOM ITALIA (TIM)

Board meeting on call for shareholders' meeting with proposals on changes to articles of association, corporate purpose.

MFE-MEDIAFOREUROPE

The executive and supervisory boards of ProSiebenSat.1 have recommended not to accept a takeover offer from MFE-MediaForEurope, the TV broadcaster controlled by Italy's Berlusconi family, the German company said on Thursday.

DIARY

Rome, 5th round of nuclear talks between Iran and the U.S.

Arezzo, event "A fair and achievable ecological transition" (0745 GMT); expected attendees include Environment and Energy Security Minister Gilberto Pichetto Fratin, Leonardo CEO Roberto Cingolani.

Florence, ECB board member Philip Lane gives speech on "Inflation and Disinflation in the Euro Area" at EMU Lab-European University Institute Florence (0830 GMT).

Trento, 20th edition of the 'Economy Festival' "Risks and fatal choices. Europe at the crossroads" continues; ends on May 25. On Friday expected attendees include income agency 'Agenzia delle Entrate' Director Vincenzo Carbone, market regulator CONSOB President Paolo Savona, state lender Cassa Depositi e Prestiti (CDP) unit SIMEST CEO Regina Corradini D'Arienzo, Industry Minister Adolfo Urso, European Affairs and National Recovery and Resilience Plan (NRRP) Minister Tommaso Foti, Fincantieri CEO Roberto Folgiero, Intesa Sanpaolo Chairman Gian Maria Gros-Pietro, Justice Minister Carlo Nordio, Labour Minister Marina Calderone, Economy Undersecretary Federico Freni, Enel Chairman Paolo Scaroni, Public Administration Minister Paolo Zangrillo, welfare institute INPS President Gabriele Fava.

((Milan newsroom, e-mail: reutersitaly@thomsonreuters.com))

data.title: De Havilland Canada announces the expansion of its presence in Greece
data.description:
ATHENS, Greece, May 23, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — De Havilland Aircraft of Canada Limited (“De Havilland Canada”) is pleased to announce the launch of De Havilland Aircraft of Canada Hellas Ltd., its new wholly-owned subsidiary in Greece, further strengthening our commitment to providing exceptional customer support to our valued customers in the region. This expansion also reflects De Havilland Canada’s deep confidence in the Greek market and its strong cooperation with the Greek government and the relevant aviation authorities.

De Havilland Canada Hellas will also function as a strategic base for DHC’s regional operations, providing closer proximity and more responsive support to operators of De Havilland Canada’s Waterbomber aircraft across Greece. This expansion underscores De Havilland Canada’s ongoing commitment to strengthening its global customer support network and ensuring the highest levels of operational efficiency and customer success. It also reinforces the company’s long-term dedication to its customers and DHC’s commitment to operators in Greece to best support their operations for decades to come.

“We are proud to establish a regional presence in Greece, a country with a strong aviation tradition. This announcement reinforces our commitment to operators in this important market,” said Jean-Philippe Côté, Vice President, Programs and Business Improvement at De Havilland Canada. “This investment will allow us to better address the specific needs of our customers in the region, delivering faster response times, improved parts availability, and personalized support.”

Marking a key step forward in their collaboration, Mr. Côté is visiting Athens this week for high-level talks with Greek government officials. Joined by Canada’s Ambassador to Greece, Anna-Karine Asselin, he is meeting with Deputy Minister for Civil Protection Evangelos Tournas and Alternate Minister of National Economy and Finance Nikos Papathanasis. The visit aims to deepen the growing partnership and identify new opportunities for future cooperation.

About De Havilland Aircraft of Canada LimitedWith more than 5,000 aircraft delivered, De Havilland Aircraft of Canada Limited (De Havilland Canada) is well established across the globe and our talented team of aviation professionals is dedicated to advancing our near-100-year reputation for excellence in innovation, production, and customer support. Our aircraft operate reliably in some of the world’s harshest climates and provide vital connections between rural communities and urban centres – transporting essential cargo and millions of passengers annually. Our aircraft also support a wide variety of special mission operations including aerial firefighting, search and rescue, medical evacuation, reconnaissance, and coastal surveillance. Visit: dehavilland.com.

For media inquiries or further information, please contact:De Havilland Canada

communications@dehavilland.com

data.title: Australia 10-Year Bond Yield Holds Decline
data.description:
Australia’s 10-year government bond yield held its recent decline to around 4.45% on Friday amid growing expectations of more policy easing by the Reserve Bank of Australia.

On Tuesday, the RBA cut its cash rate by 25bps to a two-year low of 3.85%, citing progress in reducing inflation and heightened economic risks from US tariffs.

The central bank also indicated a willingness to cut rates again if the economic outlook deteriorates sharply.

Currently, markets are implying a better than 50% chance that the RBA will lower rates again at its next meeting in July, up from less than 20% a week ago, with rates now seen to bottom at 3.1% rather than 3.35%.

Meanwhile, in the US, Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said on Thursday that the Fed could cut interest rates in the second half of 2025 if President Donald Trump's tariffs on US trading partners settle around 10%.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Bond Market Shows Signs of Stabilizing
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

Germany GDP; France consumer confidence; U.K. retail sales; no major corporate trading updates expected

Opening Call:

European stock futures rose early Friday. Asian stock benchmarks advanced; the dollar weakened; Treasury yields were little changed; while oil futures edged lower and gold inched higher.

Equities:

European stock futures were higher as worries about a global bond selloff eased.

A selloff in global sovereign bonds this week took investors by surprise, although none of the issues they appeared to be taking issue with are recent developments.

While countries like the U.S. and Japan have carried heavy debt loads for decades, Moody's decision to strip the U.S. of its top-tier credit rating earlier this month appeared to set the selloff in motion.

With U.S. growth expectations souring and the Federal Reserve on the sidelines, it appears that the selling pressure on bonds could persist, said Jens Nordvig of Exante Data.

Forex:

Markets' reaction to rising fiscal uncertainty in the U.S. might be more harmful to the dollar than to Treasurys, Deutsche Bank's Tim Baker said. "Treasuries may get some eventual support as domestics rotate away from equities, but the retreat from foreigners would still play dollar-negative."

Bonds:

Treasury yields edged lower Thursday afternoon, offering reprieve from a selloff that first gained steam on Monday, after Moody's stripped the U.S. of its triple-A credit rating last week.

"Just like with the tariffs last month, we are about to find out what level of bond yields and the S&P 500 are necessary for Trump and Congress to course-correct on what is completely unsustainable fiscal policy," said Peter Berezin, chief global strategist at BCA Research.

Energy:

Oil fell early Friday, dragged lower by the possibility of another large output increase by OPEC+ for July.

Citi sees "highly binary geopolitical risks" around U.S. negotiations on Iran's nuclear program and the Russia-Ukraine war. "Geopolitics remain pivotal for the next move in oil up to $70 Brent, or down to the $50s."

Metals:

Gold edged higher in Asian trade, supported by lingering worries over ballooning U.S. deficits.

President Trump's "big, beautiful bill" is widely expected to add trillions of dollars to budget deficits in the coming years.

-

Inventories of key metals, such as copper, have been rising in recent weeks, suggesting that demand has waned, ANZ said. However, recent supply side issues could help limit the downside, it said.

-

Iron ore prices fell slightly, as consumption is declining in China. Weakening fundamentals are weighing on the ferrous metal's prices, Baocheng Futures said.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

U.K. Consumer Sentiment Edges Up From Low Mood as Tariff Turmoil Eases

Pessimism among U.K. consumers faded a little this month, likely a result of the rollback of some of U.S. trade tariffs and the Bank of England's most recent interest-rate cut, a survey published Friday said.

Consumer confidence improved three points to minus 20 in May, according to research group GfK's monthly index published with the Nuremberg Institute for Market Decisions.

Global Finance Officials Gloss Over Trade Tensions at G-7 Summit

BANFF, Alberta-Finance officials from some of the world's most powerful nations glossed over simmering tensions triggered by President Trump's far-reaching tariffs during an economic summit in this small ski town.

At the close of the three-day meeting, the Group of Seven finance and central bank chiefs issued a vague joint statement that offered few details about what was discussed at the meetings and the commitments countries were prepared to make on trade. The joint statement mentioned the word "trade" once and made no mention of tariffs, despite the topic being a focal point in private discussions at the conference.

The Deficit Is Unsettling Bond Traders. Here's How That Affects the Economy.

An economy facing possible indigestion from big increases in tariffs now is contending with a second headwind: potential fallout from a rise in government borrowing costs.

Congressional Republicans and the White House have plowed ahead with a package of tax cuts designed to shore up the economy and soothe jitters about the hit from tariffs. But the cost of the tax bill could be adding to investor concerns over perpetually high budget deficits.

Stellantis to Postpone Production of Electric Dodge Charger Model at Ontario Plant

Stellantis said it would delay production of its 2026 base-model electric Dodge Charger Daytona at its plant in Windsor, Ontario, while it assesses the effects of the Trump administration's tariff policies.

The automaker said other variants of the car remain on track at the plant, and the decision to postpone production of the Charger Daytona R/T will have no effect on jobs at the Windsor facility.

G-7 Makes Compromises on Trade to Reach United Front, EU Official Says

BANFF, Alberta-The Group of Seven finance and central bank chiefs have agreed on wording for a communiqué although there were compromises regarding global trade, says the European Union's top economic official.

"We managed to find a lot of common ground," Valdis Dombrovskis, the EU's commissioner for economy and productivity, told reporters on the sidelines of the G-7 meeting in the Rocky Mountain resort town of Banff. The communiqué is scheduled for release later Thursday.

Big Banks Explore Venturing Into Crypto World Together With Joint Stablecoin

The nation's biggest banks are exploring whether to team up to issue a joint stablecoin, a step intended to fend off escalating competition from the cryptocurrency industry.

The conversations have so far involved companies co-owned by JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, Wells Fargo and other large commercial banks, according to people familiar with the matter. Those include Early Warning Services, the operator of the peer-to-peer payment system Zelle, and the Clearing House, the real-time payment network.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: India's JSW Steel slightly up ahead of quarterly results
data.description:
** India's top steelmaker JSW Steel up 0.4% ahead of fourth-quarter results due later in the day

** Analysts, on avg, expect co to post a ~21% rise in profit at 15.66 billion rupees, per data compiled by LSEG

** Analysts said marginal uptick in prices of the alloy and a drop in iron ore and coking coal costs — key steelmaking raw materials — to lift earnings for steelmakers

** Emkay Research analysts say JSTL's project ramp-ups during the quarter will further support its earnings in Q4

** Rival Tata Steel reported bigger-than-expected Q4 profit on lower input costs, while Jindal Steel posted a surprise loss due to one-off impairment charges

** JSTL rated "hold" on avg, while TISC, JNSP rated "buy" - LSEG data

** JSTL up 3.5% YTD vs 5% rise in benchmark Nifty 50

data.title: OpenAI Eyes Stargate Expansion in Asia Pacific
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

OpenAI is eyeing Asia for the potential expansion of the Stargate project, as global tech companies continue to boost their data-center footprints in the region amid a boom in demand for artificial-intelligence computing.

The company's Chief Strategy Officer Jason Kwon will be touring the region next week to discuss potentially expanding its "OpenAI for Countries program," an initiative within Stargate.

Kwon will be meeting government officials and potentially private-sector partners to discuss opportunities to build AI infrastructure and "deliver AI services to people" in Asia Pacific, OpenAI said in a statement Thursday.

The program, which was announced in May, is part of OpenAI's ongoing efforts to work with various countries to build their own Stargate projects rooted in "democratic AI". This includes helping them build their own data center capacity, and providing customized ChatGPT capabilities localized to the country's language and culture.

Kwon is set to visit South Korea, Singapore, Australia, India and Japan as part of the trip.

This comes amid OpenAI's announcement of Stargate UAE, where it is partnering with United Arab Emirates firm G42 and others to build a large artificial-intelligence data center in Abu Dhabi, the company's first large-scale project outside the U.S.

The Stargate project is a venture with funders including Oracle and Softbank, which plans to invest up to $500 billion over the next four years building new AI infrastructure for OpenAI in the U.S.

OpenAI has been steadily growing its presence in the Asia Pacific region. In October, it announced plans to open its second Asian office in Singapore, and has been actively hiring new staff members.

News Corp, owner of The Wall Street Journal and Dow Jones Newswires, has a content-licensing partnership with OpenAI.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: Nestle's Nescafe Earmarks Another BRL500 Million for Brazil Business
data.description:
Nestlé's Nescafé is looking to invest another 500 million Brazilian reais into its business in Brazil by 2028, Reuters reported Thursday, citing the Swiss conglomerate.

The additional investment will be used specifically to expand the Montes Claros factory in Minas Gerais and boost the presence of the Nestle Professional coffee machines.

The investment will be in addition to the 1 billion reais announced by the company in 2024.

data.title: Rich Capital Trims Attributable Loss in Fiscal H2
data.description:
Rich Capital trimmed its attributable loss to the owners of the company during the fiscal second half ended March 31 to SG$607,000 from SG$659,000 a year earlier, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Loss per share was reduced to SG$0.000008 compared with SG$0.00009 in the comparable period.

Revenue from continuing operations declined 83% year over year to SG$347,000 from SG$2.1 million a year earlier.

data.title: Hexaware Technologies Opens New Office in Chicago, US
data.description:
IT services provider Hexaware Technologies has opened a new office in Chicago, US, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The new office is expected to help the company to scale more effectively in the Midwest region of the US.

data.title: India's Bharat Electronics, Trent gain on inclusion in blue-chip Sensex
data.description:
** India's Bharat Electronics and Trent gain 1% and 2.3%, respectively

** BAJE and TREN to replace IndusInd Bank and Nestle India on BSE Sensex (BSESN), effective June 23, exchange operator said on Thursday

** Sensex inclusion to result in inflows worth $304 million for TREN and $354 million for BAJE, IIFL Securities estimates

** NEST and INBK to see outflows worth $220 million and $134 million on exclusion - IIFL

** INBK and NEST trading flat

data.title: Murban crude prices drop as OPEC+ raises output, prompting surge in volumes to Asia
data.description:
Key points:
  • Asian buyers flock to Murban as ample supply depress prices
  • Cheap Murban to squeeze Asia's demand for U.S. WTI
  • Murban becomes cheapest among grades used to assess Platts Dubai benchmark
  • Record 20 Murban cargoes delivered in Platts window in May so far

Spot premiums for Murban crude have hit six-month lows on surging supply as the United Arab Emirates ramps up output following OPEC+'s decision to accelerate production hikes and compete head-on with U.S. shale oil, traders and analysts said.

Asian buyers are flocking to the flagship Abu Dhabi light grade on low prices, with a record 10 million barrels of Murban traded on the S&P Global Platts Market on Close (MoC) process this month. Cheap Murban crude is also squeezing U.S. West Texas Intermediate oil out of Asian markets.

"Asian markets have been oversupplied with light grades for most of this cycle, driven by outages at Japanese refiners, increased UAE availability this cycle following the accelerated OPEC+ unwind, and planned maintenance at Saudi Arabia's Petro Rabigh refinery," said Richard Jones, a crude oil analyst at Energy Aspects.

"Dubai had effectively become a light benchmark this cycle," he added.

Murban became the cheapest among a handful of medium-sour crude grades that can be delivered during the Platts MoC process that sets the Dubai benchmark price, market sources said, leading to record deliveries on the platform this month.

Price weakness in the grade that accounts for two-thirds of Abu Dhabi National Oil Co's (ADNOC.UL) production is weighing on the Middle East benchmark that prices more than 14 million barrels per day of exports to Asia.

OPEC+, which includes the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and allies such as Russia, agreed to increase production by nearly 1 million barrels per day (bpd) in April, May and June, with UAE's production rising to 3.09 million bpd in June from 3.02 million bpd in May.

ADNOC expects Murban exports to exceed 1.7 million bpd in June through August, up from 1.65 million bpd in May, according to an April report on the ICE Futures Abu Dhabi (IFAD) website where Murban futures are traded. ADNOC declined to comment on Murban production and export volumes.

A source with knowledge of the production plan said Murban output will rise by 200,000 bpd to a record 2.1 million bpd in July.

IFAD Murban futures fell below $60 per barrel in early May for the first time since the contract started trading in 2021.

The average spot premium for July-loading Murban slipped to $1.19 per barrel this month, the lowest in six months, making Murban cheaper than heavier grades such as Qatar's al-Shaheen whose term price was set at a premium of $1.63 a barrel and Oman whose premium averaged $1.37 so far this month.

Heavier grades are typically cheaper than light ones as they produce more residue. The switch in pricing is partly driven by record fuel oil margins, said June Goh, senior analyst at Sparta Commodities.

A record 20 Murban cargoes have been delivered in the Platts MoC process so far this month, S&P Global said. PetroChina is the biggest buyer this month, with trading house Vitol (VITOLV.UL) delivering most of the cargoes, trade data showed.

Japan, Thailand, India and China are Asia's top Murban buyers.

Meanwhile, U.S. WTI is landing about $1 per barrel more expensive than Murban into Asia for August delivery, Sparta's Goh said, deterring Asian buyers from taking more U.S. light oil.

data.title: Elementos Completes Private Placement With Metals X; Shares Up 6%
data.description:
Elementos completed a AU$5 million private placement to Metals X , issuing 58.3 million shares at AU$0.085 per share, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company appointed current Metals X executive director Brett Smith and Chief Financial Officer Daniel Broughton as additional directors, in line with the agreement with Metals X, the filing said.

Elementos shares climbed 6% in afternoon trade Friday, while Metals X shares rose nearly 1%.
